title: Samsung Galaxy A37 and Galaxy A57 are now on sale in the US
description: The Samsung Galaxy A37 and Galaxy A57, unveiled last month, are now on sale in the US and can be purchased through Samsung's official US website. The Samsung Galaxy A57 comes in a lone Awesome Navy color with two memory options - 8GB/128GB and 8GB/256GB, priced at $550 (€470/INR51,010) and $610 (€520/INR56,575), respectively. Samsung Galaxy A57 The Samsung Galaxy A37, on the other hand, is available in two colors - Awesome Lavender and Awesome Charcoal. Its base model, priced at $450 (€385/INR41,735), has 6GB of RAM and 128GB of storage. But there's also a version with 8GB RAM...

title: Samsung Galaxy A37 review
Spotify will let you easily turn off video content across all devices
description: Spotify is introducing new video controls, which basically let you fully switch off video content in the app. This will start rolling out globally later this month and will apply to all accounts, including free ones. You'll be able to go to Settings > Content and display where you will find options to switch off Canvas looping visuals, as well as videos for music or podcasts. Once you have chosen a new preference, it will be remembered across mobile, desktop, web, TV - wherever you use Spotify. You'll still get video ads if you're on the free plan, and Canvas-like visuals on some...

title: Samsung is boosting Galaxy S26 and Galaxy S26 Ultra production
description: According to a new report from Korea, Samsung is raising production for the Galaxy S26 series this month. The company initially planned to manufacture a total of 2.4 million units in April, of which 800,000 would be the S26, 300,000 would be the S26+, and 1.3 million would be the S26 Ultra. [#InlinePriceWidget,14456,1#] [#InlinePriceWidget,14457,1#] [#InlinePriceWidget,14320,1#] However, it seems like the Galaxy S26 is a surprise hit, the S26 Ultra is selling a tiny bit better than expected, and the S26+ is selling less than Samsung would want. The new, updated production numbers...

title: Motorola Razr 70 renders leak too
description: Today has been filled with Motorola leaks - the Motorola Edge 70 Pro and Razr 70 Ultra both got portrayed in official-looking renders, and now it's time for the 'vanilla' Razr 70 to get the same treatment. The Razr 70 will be offered in four colors, allegedly, but we only have three to look at today: Pantone Sporting Green, Pantone Hematite, and Pantone Violet Ice. Motorola Razr 70 in Pantone Sporting Green The phone looks like the Razr 60 it's going to replace. The Razr 70 is rumored to come with a 6.9-inch 1080x2640 inner folding screen and a 3.63-inch 1056x1066 cover...

title: Honor's new video teaser for the 600 and 600 Pro showcases their design
description: Honor is continuing its teaser campaign for the upcoming Honor 600 and Honor 600 Pro. The brand's global X account has posted the quick video you can see embedded below, which portrays both devices in a whiteish colorway. Ready for its close-up. ✨Get an exclusive first look at the elegant curves and stunning details of the HONOR 600. The countdown begins! pic.twitter.com/QuJBTEDbyB— HONOR (@Honorglobal) April 9, 2026 The Honor 600 and 600 Pro devices are being fully unveiled on April 23, when they will join the already launched Honor 600 Lite. The Honor 600 is powered by the Snapdragon...

title: New Motorola Razr 70 Ultra press renders are here
description: CAD renders of the upcoming Motorola Razr 70 Ultra surfaced late last month, revealing the clamshell foldable in a silver shade. Now, a new set of press renders has surfaced online, showcasing the phone in two new color options. The leaked press renders showcase the Razr 70 Ultra in Orient Blue Alcantara and Pantone Cocoa Wood finishes. The former appears to feature a faux leather rear panel, while the latter adopts a matte wooden texture. A closer look at the images reveals the absence of a selfie camera on the inner folding display. This is likely an oversight, as earlier CAD...

title: Oppo Find X9 Ultra full camera details officially confirmed, design leaks
description: The Oppo Find X9 Ultra is set to debut in China and global markets on April 21. Ahead of the launch, Oppo has revealed the phone’s full camera specifications, while its design and other key specs have surfaced via a China Telecom listing. Oppo recently confirmed that the Find X9 Ultra will feature a 50MP periscope telephoto camera with 10x optical zoom, and has now revealed the rest of the device’s camera specs through a social media post. It will feature a 200MP primary sensor with an almost 1-inch size, said to deliver 10% better light intake than the Find X8 Ultra. The phone will...

title: Ai+ PulseTab arrives with 10.95” LCD and 8,000mAh battery
description: Alongside a trio of new smartphones, Ai+ also unveiled its first tablet. PulseTab brings entry-level specs, a 10.95-inch LCD and an 8,000mAh battery. It boots nxtQ OS based on Android 16 and will be available in India from May. PulseTab is built around a 10.95-inch IPS LCD with FHD+ resolution and a 90Hz refresh rate. The panel is rated at up to 400 nits peak brightness and is flanked by quad speakers. PulseTab features a 13MP rear camera and an 8MP front-facing shooter. It is equipped with MediaTek’s Helio G88 chipset paired with 6GB RAM and 128GB storage. You also get support for...

title: Here's when Samsung might unveil its 2026 Galaxy foldables
description: Samsung unveiled the Galaxy S26 series smartphones in February, and the next big smartphone announcement from the Korean brand is expected to be the Galaxy Z Fold8, Galaxy Z Flip8, and a wide foldable, which could be called Galaxy Z Fold Wide (or Fold8 Wide). There's no word from Samsung on the launch dates of these foldables, but according to Korea Economic TV, they will be unveiled on July 22. Korea Economic TV reports that these Galaxy foldables will be unveiled at a Galaxy Unpacked event in London, UK, without specifying the event time. Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8's leaked CAD-based...

title: Motorola Edge 70 Pro's leaked images reveal color options
description: Recently leaked marketing materials of the Motorola Edge 70 Pro revealed its design. Now we are looking at the leaked renders of the Edge 70 Pro, showing the smartphone in three new colors. One of these models features a wooden design on the back cover, but there's no confirmation on whether it's a real wood texture or just a design. The name of this model isn't known either. Motorola Edge 70 Pro's leaked renders However, the source reports that the other two models are called Pantone Titan and Pantone Zinfandel. It also claims that the blue version's back panel has a...

title: Ai+ unveils Nova 2 series and Nova Flip foldable
description: Indian tech brand Ai+ announced a trio of new smartphones at a big launch event today. Nova Flip is the brand’s first foldable phone, opting for a flip-style design. Nova 2 and Nova 2 Ultra are conventional slab phones with 6,000mAh batteries, 5G connectivity and 50MP main cams. Ai+ Nova Flip This is the first foldable phone from the brand, and at first glance, it sure looks like a Nubia Flip 2. A closer examination of its specs reveals that’s the case, with the only exception being that the Nova Flip runs on nxtQ OS based on Android 15. Outside of the software department,...

title: Oppo Find X9s Pro design and color options leaked
description: Ahead of its scheduled launch later this month, official-looking renders of the Oppo Find X9s Pro have surfaced online, revealing the phone in four color options. The handset is set to debut alongside the Oppo Find X9 Ultra on April 21. Reliable tipster Evan Blass has shared design renders of the Find X9s Pro on X. At first glance, the phone appears largely similar to the existing Find X9 Pro. It features the same rectangular rear camera module and a flat back panel with Oppo branding at the bottom. On the front, the device sports slim display bezels and a hole-punch cutout for the...

title: Samsung expands One UI 8.5 beta program to the Galaxy S23, Fold5, Flip5, and A36
description: Samsung has just announced that it's expanding the One UI 8.5 beta program to more devices. The beta is now available to the Galaxy S23 series (including the Galaxy S23 FE), the Galaxy Z Fold5, the Galaxy Z Flip5, and the Galaxy A36. The beta program for these phones will be available in select markets, including India, Korea, the UK, and the US. If you are in one of the supported markets, you should see a banner inside the Samsung Members app advertising the beta program, but do note that this is a phased rollout, so it might take a while to reach you. The One UI 8.5 beta now has...

title: Realme C100 4G debuts with 8,000mAh battery and IP69K rating
description: The Realme C100 series has been expanding over the last few weeks and the latest addition is the C100 4G. It brings a familiar design, MediaTek’s Helio G92 Max chipset and a massive 8,000mAh battery, which is even larger than the one on the C100 5G. Realme C100 4G Realme claims the C100 4G can deliver over 23 hours of video streaming, 20+ hours of navigation or 13+ hours of gaming on a single charge. When you do need to recharge, C100 4G supports up to 45W wired charging. Like the other C100 members, C100 4G brings reverse wired charging support with up to 10W speeds for charging...

title: Insta360 Snap adds a magnetic touchscreen viewfinder to your phone
description: Insta360’s latest accessory is a magnetically attached 3.5-inch touchscreen with two key features. It serves as a viewfinder for your rear cameras and also mirrors your phone’s screen, allowing you to access all apps and menus on your Android or iOS device. Insta360 Snap comes in two varieties – a standard version priced at $80/€86/£73 and a $90/€96/£83 one, which adds an adjustable LED light. Both connect via USB-C and draw power from your phone. Insta360 claims this minimizes lag to as low as 30ms, which is much better than competing Bluetooth options. ...

title: Apple to use foldable panels made by Samsung Display
description: According to a new report out of Korea, Apple is expected to exclusively source foldable screens from Samsung Display. These foldable OLED panels will likely be used in the company’s upcoming iPhone Fold. Korean publication The Elec reports, citing industry sources, that Samsung Display will supply Apple with its foldable OLED panels for the next three years. During the period, Apple will not be using foldable panels from other suppliers, the report stated. Samsung Display is said to begin production of the OLED foldable screens in the 2nd quarter with initial shipments expected to...

title: This may be your last chance to get a Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold
description: Samsung has already discontinued the Galaxy Z TriFold in Korea, but it still has some stock left for the US. That said, the device's discontinuation in the US market is also imminent. So if you want one, but haven't so far managed to grab it because of how quickly it has repeatedly gone out of stock, you will have another chance soon. On April 10, Samsung is restocking the device in the US, both in its online store and also at its physical stores in Cerritos, CA, Bloomington, MN, Elmhurst, NY, Garden City, NY, Glendale, CA, Houston, TX, and Frisco, TX. Do keep in mind that so far...

title: Google Chrome gets vertical tabs and immersive reading mode
description: Google Chrome on desktop is getting two long-awaited features. These included vertical tabs as well as a new immersive reading mode. Both of these features have been available on other browsers for a while now so Chrome is late to the party as usual, but better late than never. The vertical tabs feature is exactly what it sounds like. Instead of having all tabs appear in a row at the top of the browser, they appear in a vertical stack on the side. This allows you to see a lot more tabs at once than what can fit in a horizontal row, and the column can be collapsed to take less...

title: Here's the Motorola Edge 70 Pro in leaked marketing materials
description: The Edge 70 Pro is one of three Edge 70 devices still left to be launched according to a recent rumor, and today a couple of purported marketing images showing it have been outed by a tipster over on X. As you can see, the tagline for the device is going to be "seize the night", which makes us think Motorola will heavily market its camera abilities, especially for nighttime photography. The handset will be offered in three colorways, depicted in the image above, though these don't match up with what was recently leaked, so as always don't forget to take any rumor (including this...
